name: Sierra Bermeja — circular desde Mirandilla
region_intro: Jogging around Mirandilla provides access to the natural landscapes of Extremadura, particularly the areas surrounding Cornalvo Natural Park. The terrain features a mix of gentle hills and reservoir paths, offering varied running experiences. Routes often traverse natural areas with open spaces and some wooded sections, providing diverse options for runners. Best jogging routes around Mirandilla The most popular jogging route is Cornalvo Reservoir Trail, a 4.7 miles (7.5 km) trail that takes 51 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the contours of the reservoir. Another top favourite among local runners is Trujillanos Circular Route, a moderate 8.6 miles (13.8 km) path. This route features a mix of flat sections and gentle ascents, often completed in about 1 hour 40 minutes. Local runners also love the Sierra Bermeja — circular desde Mirandilla, a 7.3 miles (11.7 km) trail leading through the Sierra Bermeja hills, often completed in about 1 hour 25 minutes. Jogging around Mirandilla is defined by reservoir-side paths, rolling hills, and open natural areas. The network offers options for both moderate and more challenging running routes. The routes in Mirandilla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 800 runners have used komoot to explore Mirandilla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Mirandilla?
A: There are over 30 running routes available around Mirandilla, offering a variety of experiences for different fitness levels. You'll find options ranging from moderate to difficult.

Q: Are there easy running routes for beginners in Mirandilla?
A: While the guide primarily features moderate to difficult routes, many of the trails around Mirandilla, especially those along reservoir paths, can be adapted for beginners by choosing shorter sections or focusing on the flatter parts. Currently, there are no routes explicitly categorized as 'easy' in our selection.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in the Mirandilla area?
A: Yes, Mirandilla offers several excellent circular running routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1280358085/trujillanos-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trujillanos Circular Route&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 8.6-mile (13.8 km) path. Another option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1280321150/sierra-bermeja-circular-desde-mirandilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Bermeja — circular desde Mirandilla&lt;/a&gt;, a 7.3-mile (11.7 km) trail.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging trails near Mirandilla?
A: The jogging routes around Mirandilla feature diverse terrain, including reservoir-side paths, rolling hills, and open natural areas. You'll encounter a mix of flat sections and gentle ascents, particularly within and around Cornalvo Natural Park.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly running trails around Mirandilla?
A: Many of the natural trails around Mirandilla, especially those within Cornalvo Natural Park, are generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash and be mindful of local regulations, especially concerning wildlife and livestock.

Q: What are some points of interest or attractions I can see while jogging near Mirandilla?
A: While jogging, you might encounter natural beauty like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3071953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The wider region also boasts historical sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3775017" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Temple of Diana (Mérida)&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3813989" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Milagros Aqueduct&lt;/a&gt;, though these might require a slight detour from the immediate running paths.

Q: Is there parking available near the running routes in Mirandilla?
A: While specific parking details for each route vary, many trailheads, especially those near natural parks or reservoirs like Cornalvo, typically offer designated parking areas. It's advisable to check the route details on komoot for specific parking information before you go.

Q: How long do the running routes around Mirandilla typically take to complete?
A: The duration of running routes in Mirandilla varies. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992978510/cornalvo-reservoir-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Reservoir Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.7 miles (7.5 km), takes approximately 51 minutes. Longer routes, like the 8.6-mile (13.8 km) &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1280358085/trujillanos-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trujillanos Circular Route&lt;/a&gt;, can take around 1 hour 40 minutes.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging routes in Mirandilla?
A: The jogging routes in Mirandilla are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 800 runners have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the natural landscapes and well-maintained paths.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Yes, for experienced runners seeking a challenge, Mirandilla offers several difficult routes. An example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1280289157/el-rugidero-y-la-presa-de-cornalvo-circular-desde-la-presa-de-cornalvo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Rugidero y la Presa de Cornalvo — circular desde la Presa de Cornalvo&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 11.4-mile (18.3 km) route with significant elevation changes.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Mirandilla?
A: Given its location in Extremadura, the best time for running in Mirandilla is typically during the cooler months of spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). Summers can be very hot, making early morning or late evening runs more suitable, while winters are generally mild.

Q: Are there family-friendly running options in Mirandilla?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations are not provided, many of the moderate routes with gentler terrain, especially those around the reservoirs, can be suitable for families looking for a pleasant outdoor experience. Always consider the distance and elevation when choosing a route for children.

Q: Can I access running routes in Mirandilla using public transport?
A: Information on direct public transport access to specific trailheads in Mirandilla is limited. It is generally recommended to have private transportation to reach the starting points of many running routes, especially those located within Cornalvo Natural Park or more rural areas.
tips: The Sierra Bermeja is one of the rounded mountains that appear to the north of Mirandilla and which is topped by the Cerro del Moro.
